message:
feat(charmed): demo config and terminal IO tests

- config.rs: Demo showcase configuration improvements
- terminal_io_tests.rs: New terminal I/O test suite
- Beads issue tracker sync

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -227,15 +227,15 @@
 {"id":"bd-eo3i","title":"demo_showcase: asset strategy (docs, fixtures, icons)","description":"Decide where demo content lives and how it is loaded.

Constraints:
- Keep the repo tidy: no random extra files at root.
- Avoid runtime network dependencies.
- E2E tests must be deterministic (no dependency on the developer’s home directory).

We need assets for:
- Docs page markdown (multi-section, includes code blocks, tables, links).
- Wizard copy (prompts, tooltips, validation errors).
- Files page fixtures (small deterministic directory tree with:
  - a sample config file
  - a sample log file
  - a sample markdown file for preview
  - a couple nested folders + hidden file edge cases)
- Optional: small icon glyph reference / ASCII art.

Options:
- Embed via `include_str!` / `include_bytes!` (simple, zero runtime I/O).
- Or keep `crates/demo_showcase/assets/` and load at runtime (more editable, but requires I/O and paths).
- Hybrid: embed for runtime, but keep sources in assets for readability.

Acceptance:
- We can ship rich docs/content without external dependencies.
- E2E scenarios (especially Files) can rely on deterministic fixtures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:31.900402267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:48:37.276212852Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:48:37.276140708Z","close_reason":"Implemented asset strategy: assets.rs module with compile-time embedding via include_str!, VirtualEntry tree for FilePicker demo, docs for glamour, fixtures for file browser. Created assets/ directory with docs, config, logs, and nested fixtures.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["assets","build","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-eo3i","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:07.092907319Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-eo3i","depends_on_id":"bd-szmb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:31.916230662Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-fdf3","title":"Files: integrate bubbles FilePicker","description":"Implement file browsing using `bubbles::filepicker`.

Requirements:
- Start directory selection:
  - Default to a deterministic demo fixture directory (so screenshots/E2E are stable).
  - Allow overriding via Config to browse a real path.
- Respect hidden files toggle.

Notes:
- Tie this to the project’s asset strategy: the Files page should have a small built-in fixture tree to showcase preview/rendering and to support CI tests.

Acceptance:
- Keyboard navigation works; selection updates preview.
- In headless/E2E mode, the same file list is available every run.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:17.806118901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:00:37.807916040Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:00:37.807845619Z","close_reason":"FilePicker is fully integrated in pages/files.rs with virtual fixture tree for E2E stability, navigation (j/k/l/h), hidden files toggle, and preview pane. Fixed style method compatibility. 7 tests passing.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","demo_showcase","files"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fdf3","depends_on_id":"bd-201t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:17.835140905Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-fdf3","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:09.118398463Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-fdf3","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:00:50.071201987Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-fdf3","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:11.694843283Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-fdf3","depends_on_id":"bd-eo3i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:00:51.371150556Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-gbps","title":"Unit tests: Config parsing + env/CLI precedence","description":"Add focused unit tests for demo_showcase runtime configuration.

Why:
- The demo will have many toggles; config bugs create confusing UX and flaky tests.
- We want deterministic, documented precedence rules.

Test coverage (minimum):
- Defaults: Config created with no args/env is sane.
- CLI parsing: each flag maps correctly into Config.
- Precedence rules:
  - CLI overrides env where sensible.
  - `NO_COLOR` forces no-color unless explicitly overridden (if we support `--force-color`).
  - `--theme-file` must override `--theme` preset.
- Validation: invalid values (bad seed, bad theme name) produce clean errors.

Acceptance:
- Tests do not require a TTY.
- Failures include actionable assertions/messages.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:12.286603748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:16:20.828954820Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gbps","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:12.307911148Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-gbps","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:12.302950583Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-gp8c","title":"Fix Wish keyboard-interactive auth handling and session manager safety","description":"Fix Wish auth keyboard-interactive flow (russh handler missing), ensure session cleanup task is single-shot, and prevent channel count underflow.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:49:37.934158444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:49:51.125584226Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:49:51.125513313Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gyzr","title":"Code Quality & Reliability Hardening: Deep Review Findings","description":"# Epic: Code Quality & Reliability Hardening

## Origin
This epic captures all deficiencies identified during a comprehensive deep-dive code review
of the entire charmed_rust codebase conducted on 2026-01-27. The review examined all core
crates (bubbletea, lipgloss, bubbles, glamour, huh, charmed_log, harmonica) for:

- Bugs and correctness issues
- Thread safety and concurrency problems
- Performance inefficiencies
- API design issues
- Code duplication and maintainability

## Scope
The findings span multiple crates with varying severity:
- **CRITICAL**: Issues that can cause panics, data loss, or security problems
- **HIGH**: Issues affecting correctness or significant performance
- **MEDIUM**: Code quality, maintainability, and minor performance issues

## Goals
1. Eliminate all sources of unexpected panics (lock poisoning, bounds checks)
2. Ensure reliable I/O with proper error handling
3. Consolidate duplicated implementations for maintainability
4. Optimize hot paths identified during review
5. Fix all identified bugs and edge cases

## Dependencies
This epic's tasks should be completed before major feature work to ensure a stable foundation.

## Quality Bar
Each fix must:
- Include regression tests where applicable
- Pass all existing tests
- Pass clippy with -D warnings
- Be reviewed for thread safety implications","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:14:47.042212490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:08:44.623313430Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:08:44.623247147Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["code-quality","hardening","review-findings"]}
 {"id":"bd-i0i9","title":"Docs: docs navigation (list of pages + preview)","description":"Implement a docs index + navigation experience.

Design:
- Split view:
  - Left pane: doc page list (filterable list)
  - Right pane: rendered content (glamour + viewport)

Behavior:
- Switching docs is instant.
- Preserve per-doc scroll position when switching away and back (optional but high polish).
- The list should reflect the authored docs content (title/subtitle) rather than raw filenames.

Integration:
- Hooks into in-doc search (search operates within the current doc).

Acceptance:
- Navigation feels fast and "real product" quality.
- Selection and (optionally) scroll state are preserved across page navigations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:43.361710616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:13:42.516077791Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:13:42.516008842Z","close_reason":"Implemented split-view docs navigation with left pane document list and right pane content viewer. Features include: Tab/Arrow keys to switch focus between panes, j/k navigation in list, vim-style scrolling in content, per-document scroll position preservation, and theme-aware rendering with highlighted borders.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","list"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-i0i9","depends_on_id":"bd-22vc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:43.387706158Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-i0i9","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:26.023997036Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ikfq","title":"Unit Tests: bubbletea simulator","description":"Comprehensive tests for the bubbletea test simulator.

## Current State
Simulator has tests but needs expansion for:
- Batch/sequence command execution
- Timer and tick simulation
- Window resize handling
- Error propagation

## Test Requirements

### Simulator Core
- [ ] sim_init() with various Model implementations
- [ ] sim_update() message routing
- [ ] sim_view() output capture
- [ ] Multiple update cycles

### Event Simulation
- [ ] sim_key() for all key types
- [ ] sim_mouse() for all mouse events
- [ ] sim_paste() for clipboard
- [ ] sim_resize() dimension handling

### Command Execution
- [ ] Batch commands execute all
- [ ] Sequence commands maintain order
- [ ] Nested batch/sequence combinations
- [ ] Command result message routing

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Empty batch handling
- [ ] Recursive updates (message spawns message)
- [ ] Large message queues
- [ ] Concurrent timer expirations

## Success Criteria
- Simulator can test any Model without real terminal
- All command types work correctly
- Timing-sensitive tests are deterministic","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:35.290507703Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:38:44.380704888Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:38:42.845133735Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","simulator","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ikfq","depends_on_id":"bd-28co","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:35.310938715Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":28,"issue_id":"bd-ikfq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETED by VioletDog (2026-01-28)

## Summary
Added comprehensive tests and helper methods to the bubbletea simulator.

## New Helper Methods (5)
- `sim_key(c: char)` - Simulate single character key press
- `sim_key_type(key_type: KeyType)` - Simulate special keys (Enter, Esc, arrows)
- `sim_mouse(x, y, button, action)` - Simulate mouse events
- `sim_resize(width, height)` - Simulate window resize
- `sim_paste(text)` - Simulate bracketed paste

## New Tests (12)
### Event Simulation
- test_sim_key_sends_char
- test_sim_key_type_sends_special_keys
- test_sim_mouse_sends_clicks
- test_sim_resize_sends_dimensions
- test_sim_paste_sends_text

### Sequence Commands
- test_simulator_sequence_command

### Edge Cases
- test_empty_batch_does_not_panic
- test_recursive_updates_bounded
- test_large_message_queue
- test_model_mut_allows_direct_modification
- test_step_without_messages_returns_none
- test_views_accumulate

## Test Results
**22/22 simulator tests pass**","created_at":"2026-01-28T20:38:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iwbw","title":"Dashboard: animated counters/progress (harmonica)","description":"Make the Dashboard feel alive with tasteful, physics-based motion (harmonica).

Why:
- Smooth motion is a signature Charm aesthetic.
- Animated counters and progress bars make metrics changes readable and satisfying.

What to animate:
- Counter values (requests/sec, latency, error rate):
  - spring toward target to avoid jitter
- Progress bars:
  - ease changes rather than snapping
- Optional:
  - a subtle transition when switching focus between dashboard cards

Constraints:
- Must not interfere with input responsiveness.
- Must respect reduce-motion / `--no-animations`:
  - values snap directly to targets

Acceptance:
- Motion is smooth, stable, and never causes flicker.
- The dashboard remains readable even with rapid metric changes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:59.093043228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:56:24.359362381Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:56:24.359295086Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","harmonica"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iwbw","depends_on_id":"bd-2myg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:19.483793101Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-iwbw","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:20.705038373Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-iwbw","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:59.109088230Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-k52c","title":"demo_showcase theme: live theme switching UI","description":"Implement runtime theme switching.

- A theme picker (modal or Settings page) showing available presets.

 {"id":"bd-l26a","title":"Dashboard: implement layout (cards, grids, status)","description":"Implement the dashboard screen layout.

Content ideas:
- Top row: overall platform status, current env, uptime, active incidents.
- Middle: service health cards + deployment status.
- Bottom: small table (recent deploys/jobs) + sparkline-like text charts.

Acceptance:
- Looks great at 80x24 and scales up gracefully.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:56.862373395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:20:28.911225625Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:20:28.911161195Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","lipgloss"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l26a","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:13.068128175Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-l26a","depends_on_id":"bd-3bz7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:15.639252973Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-l26a","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:14.418099545Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-l26a","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:56.880742443Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-lxky","title":"Unit tests: animation subsystem determinism + reduce-motion","description":"Add deterministic unit tests for the demo_showcase animation subsystem.

Why:
- The showcase will lean heavily on motion; we need confidence it won’t become flaky or timing-dependent.
- CI must be able to validate animation code paths without real sleeps.

Test coverage:
- Given a fixed dt (e.g., 16ms) and fixed initial/target values, stepping the animation yields deterministic values.
- Animations stay bounded (no NaNs, no runaway values, no panics on extreme dt).
- Reduce-motion / `--no-animations` behavior:
  - animated values immediately snap to target (or the subsystem becomes a no-op) according to the design.
  - no tick scheduling when animations are disabled.

Acceptance:
- Tests do not depend on wall-clock time.
- Failures clearly report which animation invariant broke.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.665155320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.688983950Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.688967780Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.679805544Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.684774144Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-lxky","title":"Unit tests: animation subsystem determinism + reduce-motion","description":"Add deterministic unit tests for the demo_showcase animation subsystem.

Why:
- The showcase will lean heavily on motion; we need confidence it won’t become flaky or timing-dependent.
- CI must be able to validate animation code paths without real sleeps.

Test coverage:
- Given a fixed dt (e.g., 16ms) and fixed initial/target values, stepping the animation yields deterministic values.
- Animations stay bounded (no NaNs, no runaway values, no panics on extreme dt).
- Reduce-motion / `--no-animations` behavior:
  - animated values immediately snap to target (or the subsystem becomes a no-op) according to the design.
  - no tick scheduling when animations are disabled.

Acceptance:
- Tests do not depend on wall-clock time.
- Failures clearly report which animation invariant broke.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.665155320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:18:27.917920601Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.688967780Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.679805544Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.684774144Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-miqo","title":"E2E scenario: docs render + search + resize reflow","description":"Scenario: docs page rendering quality.

Steps:
- Open Docs
- Switch between two docs
- Search for a term
- Simulate resize (send WindowSizeMsg) and verify reflow occurs

Assertions:
- Rendered output changes with width.
- Search navigation finds matches.

Acceptance:
- Catches regressions in glamour integration and responsive layout.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:39.583157581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:35.534673474Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-miqo","depends_on_id":"bd-2lkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:34.234331367Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-miqo","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:32.891451084Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-miqo","depends_on_id":"bd-39bb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:35.534642717Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-miqo","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:39.598959258Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-mwty","title":"Logs: implement live log viewer (viewport + follow mode)","description":"Implement the Logs page core UI (the thing users will stare at for minutes).

Why:
- Logs are the heartbeat of an ops console.
- This is where styling + performance + interaction quality are most obvious.

Requirements:
- Viewport that can scroll back through a large buffer.
- Follow mode (tail -f) toggle:
  - when enabled, viewport stays pinned to latest entries
  - when user scrolls up, follow pauses automatically
  - a clear UI hint indicates "paused" vs "following"
- Selection (optional but great):
  - highlight current line for copy/export

Performance:
- Must handle thousands of lines without flicker.
- Avoid rebuilding huge strings unnecessarily; prefer rendering only the visible window.

Integration:
- Uses structured log entries + formatter (`Logs: styled log formatting using charmed_log`).
- Filtering/search is layered on later but the core viewer should support it cleanly.

Acceptance:
- Smooth scrolling and no flicker.
- Follow mode behavior matches user expectations.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:36.085412578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:53:36.090025763Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:53:36.089960261Z","close_reason":"Implemented LogsPage with live log viewer: viewport scrolling, follow mode (tail -f), auto-pause on scroll up, status bar with level counts and position. Added 8 tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logs","viewport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mwty","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:33.789901378Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-mwty","depends_on_id":"bd-2qx1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:36.112421707Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-mwty","depends_on_id":"bd-32pp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:36.416170919Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ndn1","title":"EPIC: Property-Based Testing (proptest)","description":"Property-based testing using proptest for algorithmic and numeric code.

## Philosophy
- Let the computer find edge cases humans miss
- Test invariants, not specific inputs
- Shrink failing cases to minimal reproductions
- Focus on numeric/algorithmic code

## Target Areas
1. harmonica - spring physics calculations
   - Animation value convergence
   - Velocity damping behavior
   - Frame timing consistency

2. lipgloss - dimension calculations
   - Width/height calculations with padding/margin
   - Position calculations
   - Unicode width handling

3. bubbles - component state machines
   - Viewport scroll bounds
   - Progress bar percentages
   - Spinner frame cycling

4. glamour - text wrapping
   - Word wrap boundaries
   - Unicode character handling
   - ANSI sequence preservation

## Property Examples
- For any input, width >= content width
- Animation always converges to target
- Scroll position always in valid range
- Progress percentage always 0-100","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:06.367379778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:59:12.009040062Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ndn1","depends_on_id":"bd-665u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:06.381903246Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":25,"issue_id":"bd-ndn1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PROPERTY TESTING STRATEGY:

Property tests complement unit tests by finding edge cases that humans miss.
They're especially valuable for:
- Numeric/mathematical code (harmonica springs)
- Dimension calculations (lipgloss widths)
- State machines (bubbles components)
- Text processing (glamour wrapping)

PROPTEST PATTERNS:

1. INVARIANT PROPERTIES - 'This must ALWAYS be true'
   ```rust
   proptest! {
       #[test]
       fn progress_always_valid(pct in any::<f64>()) {
           let p = Progress::new().set_percent(pct);
           assert!(p.percent() >= 0.0 && p.percent() <= 1.0);
       }
   }
   ```

2. ROUND-TRIP PROPERTIES - 'Encode then decode equals original'
   ```rust
   proptest! {
       #[test]
       fn style_render_preserves_content(s in "[a-z ]{0,50}") {
           let styled = Style::new().render(&s);
           let stripped = strip_ansi(&styled);
           assert_eq!(stripped.trim(), s.trim());
       }
   }
   ```

3. COMPARISON PROPERTIES - 'This equals that'
   ```rust
   proptest! {
       #[test]
       fn batch_order_independent(cmds in vec(any_cmd(), 0..10)) {
           // Results should be same regardless of batch order
       }
   }
   ```

SHRINKING: Let proptest shrink failures to minimal reproducible cases.
Always save the seed for reproducibility in CI.","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:59:12Z"}]}
